examName: AWS AIF-C01 Mock Exam - Advanced (Domain Weighted)

questions.text: A data scientist is building a model to predict whether a customer will renew their subscription. The dataset has 10,000 rows with features like usage frequency, support tickets, and account age. The target is binary (renew = yes/no). Which algorithm type is most appropriate as a baseline?
questions.explanation: Logistic regression is the standard baseline for binary classification problems.

questions.text: A model trained to detect fraudulent transactions achieves 99% accuracy on the test set. However, the dataset had only 1% fraudulent transactions, and the model predicted 'not fraud' for every transaction. What is the primary issue?
questions.explanation: In imbalanced datasets, accuracy is misleading. The model has 0% recall on the fraud class.

questions.text: Which of the following metrics would be MOST appropriate to evaluate a model for detecting rare diseases where false negatives are critical to avoid?
questions.explanation: Recall measures how many actual positives were correctly identified. For rare diseases, missing a case (false negative) is dangerous.

questions.text: A model trained on historical sales data predicts next month's revenue as a continuous number. What type of machine learning problem is this?
questions.explanation: Predicting a continuous numerical value (revenue) is regression.

questions.text: Which of the following describes the scenario where a model performs well on training data but poorly on unseen test data? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Overfitting and high variance both describe models that memorize training data but fail to generalize.

questions.text: What is the purpose of a validation set in machine learning?
options: To tune hyperparameters and select models
questions.explanation: The validation set is used during training to tune hyperparameters and select the best model.

questions.text: A team wants to group customers into segments based on purchasing behavior without predefined labels. Which approach should they use?
questions.explanation: Clustering is unsupervised learning used to find natural groupings without labels.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of reinforcement learning?
options: A game-playing AI that learns from wins and losses
Detecting spam emails from labeled examples
questions.explanation: Reinforcement learning involves an agent learning through rewards and penalties from actions.

questions.text: A company wants to generate realistic product descriptions for 10,000 items using existing catalog data. Which approach is MOST efficient?
options: Hire writers to manually create descriptions
Train a custom CNN from scratch
Use a pre-trained LLM with few-shot prompting
Build a regression model on product attributes
questions.explanation: Pre-trained LLMs with few-shot prompting are the most efficient for text generation tasks.

questions.text: Which of the following are foundation model capabilities available through Amazon Bedrock? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Bedrock offers Claude for text and Titan for image generation.

questions.text: A developer is using Amazon Bedrock with a foundation model. They notice the model sometimes generates false information about company products. What is this phenomenon called?
questions.explanation: Hallucination occurs when generative models produce factually incorrect but confident-sounding information.

questions.text: What is the primary difference between fine-tuning and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)?
options: Fine-tuning modifies model weights; RAG injects context without changing weights
RAG requires more data than fine-tuning
Fine-tuning is only for images; RAG is for text
There is no difference; they are the same
questions.explanation: Fine-tuning updates model weights; RAG retrieves relevant context and injects it into the prompt without retraining.

questions.text: Which Bedrock feature allows you to define denied topics (e.g., 'do not discuss competitor products') and block harmful content?
questions.explanation: Guardrails provide configurable safeguards including denied topics and content filtering.

questions.text: A company wants to build a Q&A system over 10,000 internal PDF documents without retraining the foundation model. Which Bedrock feature should they use?
questions.explanation: Knowledge Bases enable RAG — retrieving relevant documents and providing them as context without fine-tuning.

questions.text: A developer wants to create a workflow where an LLM can check order status by calling a company API. Which Bedrock feature enables this?
questions.explanation: Agents allow foundation models to take actions by integrating with APIs and business systems.

questions.text: Which combination of services would you use to build a voice-based customer support bot that can retrieve account information from a database?
options: Amazon Transcribe + Amazon Polly + AWS Lambda
Amazon Lex + Amazon Polly + AWS Lambda
Amazon Lex + Amazon Comprehend + Amazon S3
Amazon Transcribe + Amazon Rekognition + Amazon DynamoDB
questions.explanation: Lex handles conversation (including speech input), Polly provides speech output, and Lambda handles backend integration.

questions.text: A financial firm needs to extract data from thousands of scanned mortgage applications. The documents contain handwritten text, tables, and signatures. Which service should they use FIRST?
questions.explanation: Textract extracts text, handwriting, tables, and form data from scanned documents.

questions.text: After extracting text from scanned documents using Textract, a company wants to detect personally identifiable information (PII) like names and SSNs. Which service should they use?
questions.explanation: Amazon Comprehend detects and redacts PII from text.

questions.text: A global company receives support tickets in 20 languages. They want to route tickets to English-speaking agents. Which services are required? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Comprehend detects the language, Translate converts to English.

questions.text: A company wants to build a search engine that allows employees to ask natural language questions like 'Show me Q3 financial reports with revenue growth > 10%'. Which service is purpose-built for this?
questions.explanation: Kendra provides intelligent enterprise search with natural language understanding.

questions.text: When would you choose Amazon Personalize over building a custom recommendation model in SageMaker?
options: When you need complete control over the algorithm
When you want a fully managed service with built-in recommendation algorithms
When you need to train on images
When you need real-time inference only
questions.explanation: Personalize is purpose-built, fully managed, and optimized for recommendations without custom ML development.

questions.text: A developer is using Bedrock with a prompt: 'Translate this to French: Hello, how are you?' What type of prompting is this?
questions.explanation: Zero-shot prompting gives the task without examples.

questions.text: A prompt includes: 'Question: What is the capital of France? Answer: Paris. Question: What is the capital of Germany? Answer: Berlin. Question: What is the capital of Japan? Answer:' This demonstrates which technique?
questions.explanation: Few-shot provides examples of the desired input-output format.

questions.text: An AI model for loan approvals consistently denies applications from a specific postal code. Investigation shows the postal code has a high concentration of minority residents. Which Responsible AI principle is being violated?
questions.explanation: Fairness ensures models do not discriminate based on protected characteristics.

questions.text: A bank needs to explain to regulators why a loan was denied. Which capability helps with this?
questions.explanation: Explainability tools like SHAP or SageMaker Clarify help interpret model decisions.

questions.text: Which AWS service helps detect bias in ML models and provides explainability?
questions.explanation: SageMaker Clarify detects bias and provides explainability (feature importance, SHAP).

questions.text: Which of the following are GenAI-specific security risks? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Prompt injection and data exfiltration through model outputs are unique GenAI risks.

questions.text: What does RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback) primarily address?
options: Aligning model outputs with human preferences
questions.explanation: RLHF uses human feedback to align model behavior with human preferences and values.

questions.text: A company wants to ensure their generative AI chatbot does not produce harmful content. Which Bedrock feature should they configure?
questions.explanation: Guardrails provide configurable filters for harmful content and denied topics.

questions.domain: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: An EC2 instance needs to access an S3 bucket for training data. The admin wants to avoid storing long-term credentials on the instance. What should they do?
options: Create an IAM user and store access keys in a file
Attach an IAM role to the EC2 instance
questions.explanation: IAM roles provide temporary credentials to EC2 instances securely.

questions.domain: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: Which AWS service records API activity for auditing and compliance in AI workflows?
questions.explanation: CloudTrail records API calls for auditing, security analysis, and compliance.

questions.domain: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: A security team wants to automatically discover and protect sensitive data (like credit card numbers) stored in S3 buckets. Which service should they use?
questions.explanation: Amazon Macie uses ML to discover and protect sensitive data in S3.

questions.domain: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: Which AWS service provides self-service access to AWS compliance reports (SOC, PCI, etc.)?
questions.explanation: AWS Artifact provides on-demand access to AWS compliance reports and agreements.

questions.domain: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: A company wants to track changes to S3 bucket policies over time and ensure compliance with security standards. Which service should they use?
questions.explanation: AWS Config tracks resource configurations and evaluates compliance against desired policies.

questions.domain: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: Which of the following are IAM best practices? (Select TWO)
options: Use root user for daily tasks
Share access keys across team members
Store access keys in public repositories
questions.explanation: MFA and least privilege are core IAM security best practices.

questions.domain: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: A company using Bedrock wants to audit which users invoked which foundation models. Which service provides this information?
questions.explanation: CloudTrail records all Bedrock API calls including who invoked which model.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of MLOps?
options: Automating the ML lifecycle (CI/CD, monitoring, governance)
questions.explanation: MLOps automates and operationalizes the end-to-end ML lifecycle.

questions.text: Which combination of services would you use to process a scanned PDF containing handwritten notes, extract the text, and translate it from Spanish to English?
questions.explanation: Textract extracts text (including handwriting), Translate translates the extracted text.

questions.text: A team wants to compare Claude, Llama, and Titan models on accuracy and toxicity before selecting one for production. Which Bedrock feature should they use?
questions.explanation: Bedrock Model Evaluation allows comparing foundation models on multiple metrics.

questions.text: A media company wants to convert their podcast library (audio files) into searchable text transcripts. Which service should they use?
questions.explanation: Transcribe converts speech to text from audio files.

questions.text: A company wants to generate realistic human-like voices for their audiobook production. Which service should they use?
questions.explanation: Polly converts text to lifelike speech.

questions.text: What is the purpose of SageMaker Model Monitor?
options: To detect bias in training data
To monitor deployed models for data drift and quality issues
questions.explanation: Model Monitor detects drift in data, predictions, and model quality after deployment.

questions.domain: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: Which AWS service helps automate evidence collection for audits?
questions.explanation: AWS Audit Manager automates evidence collection and helps prepare for audits.

questions.text: What is the difference between a hyperparameter and a model parameter?
options: Hyperparameters are set before training; model parameters are learned during training
Model parameters are set before training; hyperparameters are learned
Hyperparameters are only used in neural networks
questions.explanation: Hyperparameters (e.g., learning rate) are set before training; model parameters (e.g., weights) are learned.

questions.text: When would you use Amazon Forecast instead of building a custom time-series model in SageMaker?
options: When you need full control over the algorithm
When you want a fully managed service purpose-built for forecasting
questions.explanation: Forecast is purpose-built for time-series forecasting with minimal ML expertise.

questions.text: Which of the following is a key characteristic of foundation models?
options: They are trained on a specific narrow task
They are pre-trained on broad data and can be adapted to many tasks
They only work with text data
questions.explanation: Foundation models are pre-trained on broad data and can be adapted to various downstream tasks.

questions.text: A company deploys an AI model that performs well in production for 6 months, then gradually becomes less accurate due to changing customer behavior. This is an example of:
questions.explanation: Model drift occurs when the real-world data distribution changes over time.

questions.domain: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: What does the AWS Shared Responsibility Model state for AI services?
options: AWS secures the AI service infrastructure; customer secures their data, prompts, and configurations
Security is automatically handled by AI
questions.explanation: AWS secures the infrastructure; customers secure their data, access controls, and configurations.

questions.text: Place the steps for building a RAG application with Bedrock Knowledge Bases in the correct order: 1. Query knowledge base, 2. Ingest documents, 3. Create knowledge base, 4. Configure data source from S3
questions.explanation: First create the knowledge base, configure the data source, ingest documents, then query.

questions.text: A model for predicting customer churn has high precision but low recall. What does this mean?
options: When it predicts churn, it's usually correct, but it misses many actual churn cases
It predicts churn for almost all customers
questions.explanation: High precision means few false positives; low recall means many false negatives (missed churn).

questions.text: A company wants to add a human review step for low-confidence document extractions. Which service should they use?
questions.explanation: A2I adds human review workflows to AI predictions, especially for low-confidence results.

questions.text: What is the primary benefit of using prompt templates?
options: They provide reusable, consistent prompts with placeholders
They eliminate the need for guardrails
questions.explanation: Prompt templates provide reusable structures with placeholders for variables, ensuring consistency.

questions.domain: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: Which AWS service helps identify security vulnerabilities in EC2 instances running ML workloads?
questions.explanation: Amazon Inspector scans for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure.

questions.text: A developer wants to build an application where users can describe an app they need in plain English, and the system generates a simple working app. Which Amazon Q feature supports this?
questions.explanation: Amazon Q Apps allows users to build lightweight applications from natural language descriptions.

questions.text: What is the purpose of SageMaker Experiments?
options: To track and compare training runs, hyperparameters, and metrics
questions.explanation: SageMaker Experiments tracks training runs, hyperparameters, metrics, and artifacts.

questions.text: Which of the following are use cases for Amazon Rekognition? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Rekognition handles image/video analysis including content moderation and facial recognition.

questions.text: What is the purpose of SageMaker Debugger?
options: To monitor deployed models for drift
To debug training jobs by monitoring gradients and activations
questions.explanation: SageMaker Debugger identifies training issues like vanishing gradients, overfitting, and slow training.

questions.domain: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: Which service provides recommendations on cost optimization, security, and performance for AWS accounts?
questions.explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor provides best practice recommendations across cost, security, performance, and fault tolerance.

questions.text: A prompt asks the model to 'explain your reasoning step by step before providing the final answer.' Which technique is this?
questions.explanation: Chain-of-thought prompting asks the model to reason step by step before answering.

questions.text: A company needs to forecast inventory demand for 10,000 SKUs with seasonal patterns. Which service is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Amazon Forecast is purpose-built for time-series forecasting at scale.

questions.text: Match each ML concept to its description: 1. Supervised learning, 2. Unsupervised learning, 3. Reinforcement learning, 4. Self-supervised learning
rightItems: Learning from pretext tasks on unlabeled data
questions.explanation: Supervised uses labels; unsupervised finds patterns; RL uses rewards; self-supervised creates pretext tasks.

questions.text: Match each AWS service to its primary use: 1. Amazon Kendra, 2. Amazon Lex, 3. Amazon Polly, 4. Amazon Transcribe
questions.explanation: Kendra = search, Lex = chatbots, Polly = text-to-speech, Transcribe = speech-to-text.

questions.text: Match each Responsible AI pillar to its definition: 1. Fairness, 2. Explainability, 3. Robustness, 4. Privacy
rightItems: Understanding why a model made a decision
questions.explanation: Fairness = no bias; Explainability = interpretability; Robustness = reliability; Privacy = data protection.

questions.domain: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: Match each AWS service to its security function: 1. AWS CloudTrail, 2. Amazon Macie, 3. AWS Config, 4. Amazon Inspector
questions.explanation: CloudTrail = audit, Macie = sensitive data, Config = compliance, Inspector = vulnerabilities.

questions.text: Place the steps for fine-tuning a foundation model in Bedrock in order: 1. Evaluate model, 2. Select base model, 3. Prepare training data, 4. Start fine-tuning job
questions.explanation: Select model first, prepare data, start fine-tuning, then evaluate the fine-tuned model.
